Taking advantage of their weighty armor, Molthuni defenders can hold back an onslaught of enemies intent on breaking through their line. The majority of Molthuni defenders were laborers who joined the military to gain citizenship, though a noteworthy minority are from stouter non-human races.

:::ab{l=3 icon=def-lower-boost title="Armored Defense (Ex)" action="Passive Ability" imp7="The bonus becomes +2 in medium armor and +4 in heavy armor. He can choose an additional combat maneuver to add half his *armor training* bonus to." imp11="The bonus becomes +3 in medium armor and +5 in heavy armor. He can choose a second combat maneuver to add half his *armor training* bonus to." imp15="The bonus becomes +4 in medium armor and +6 in heavy armor. He can choose a third combat maneuver to add half his *armor training* bonus to." replace="Armor training 1 through 4" special="This counts as *armor training* for the purposes of qualifying for feats and prestige classes. It does not count as *advanced armor training.*"}

A Molthuni defender gains a bonus to his CMD against bull rush, dirty trick, drag, grapple, overrun, reposition, and trip combat maneuver checks. This bonus also applies to the DC of foes' Acrobatics checks to move through squares he threatens. This bonus is +1 when the Molthuni defender is wearing medium armor, and +3 when he is wearing heavy armor.

In addition to this, the Molthuni defender is able to choose one of the following combat maneuvers: bull rush, dirty trick, drag, grapple, overrun, reposition, or trip. When he is wearing medium or heavy armor, the Molthuni defender adds ‹misc/half› his *armor training* bonus on combat maneuver checks for the selected maneuver.

mutation_warrior.name: Mutation Warrior
description: ## Mutation Warrior
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/93›
While most fighters rely on physical fitness and rigorous training to achieve martial superiority, a few prefer to create and imbibe dangerous concoctions that mutate them into fearsome creatures.

::ab[Mutagen (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A mutation warrior discovers how to create a mutagen that he can imbibe in order to heighten his physical prowess at the cost of his personality. This ability functions as the ‹class/alchemist«'s› mutagen ability, using his fighter level as his alchemist level." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Mutagen Discovery (Su)]{l=7 icon=power choice="The mutation warrior can choose one of the following alchemist discoveries to augment his abilities: ‹discovery/feral mutagen›, ‹discovery/grand mutagen›, ‹discovery/greater mutagen›, ‹discovery/infuse mutagen›, ‹discovery/nauseating flesh›, ‹discovery/preserve organs›, ‹discovery/rag doll mutagen›, ‹discovery/spontaneous healing›, ‹discovery/tentacle›, ‹discovery/vestigial arm›, ‹discovery/wings›. The mutagen warrior uses his fighter level as his effective alchemist level for the purpose of these discoveries." imp11="He can choose a second discovery from the given list." imp15="He can choose a third such discovery." imp19="He can choose a fourth such discovery." replace="Armor training 2, 3, 4~armor mastery"}

opportunist.name: Opportunist
description: ## Opportunist
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/20›

Opportunists believe every battle is one of wits rather than arms. The ratfolk enforcers who protect Goka's infamous Deepmarket embrace this philosophy, unleashing a disorienting mix of martial skill and alchemical weaponry.
::prereq{r=Ratfolk}

::ab[Duplicitous (Ex)]{icon=skill note="An opportunist adds ‹skill/Bluff› (CHA), ‹skill/Sense Motive› (WIS), ‹skill/Sleight of Hand› (DEX), and ‹skill/Stealth› (DEX) to his list of class skills. He gains 2 bonus skill ranks at each level, which must be allocated among these skills." replace=Bravery alter="Class skills"}

::ab[Underhanded (Ex)]{icon=power l=1 ability="An opportunist gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat even if he does not meet the prerequisites." replace="The bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Cunning Edge (Ex)]{head l=4 icon=power choice="An opportunist can select an edge from those listed below." imp8="He can select a second edge." imp12="He can select a third edge." imp16="He can select a fourth edge." imp20="He can select a fifth edge." replace="Bonus feats gained at 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th levels"}

::ab[Alchemical Admixture (Ex)]{next icon=power provokes=Yes fullround="The opportunist can combine two alchemical weapons into a single vial. When thrown as a splash weapon, the mixture has the effects of both component substances and targets are affected as if hit by both. The mixture becomes inert after 1 hour. Combining identical alchemical weapons has no effect." useInc="4~fighter"}

::ab[Alchemical Refinement (Ex)]{next icon=lower ability="The opportunist increases the save DC of any alchemical item he crafts by an amount equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his fighter level. Doing so increases the raw material cost to craft that item by 10 gp for every +1 increase. This bonus applies only when the opportunist uses the crafted alchemical item himself; it functions as a normal item of its type for all other users."}

::ab[Bombs (Ex)]{next icon=power-zap ability="The opportunist can make a number of bombs per day equal to his Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). These bombs act as ‹class/alchemist«'s› bombs and deal 1d6 points of fire damage, plus an additional 1d6 points for every 3 fighter levels the opportunist has."}

::ab[Clouded Shift (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="As part of the standard action to ignite a ‹eq-misc/smokestick› or ‹eq-misc/smoke pellet›, the opportunist can take a 5-foot step, even if he has already moved this round. He can use this ability only if he has not already taken a 5-foot step this round, and only if the smoke from his alchemical tool conceals his starting location."}
::ab[Poison Use (Ex)]{next icon=def ability="The opportunist no longer risks poisoning himself when applying poison to a weapon."}

::ab[Rogue Talent (Ex)]{next icon=power choice="The opportunist can select one of the following rogue talents for which he qualifies, treating his fighter level as his rogue level: ‹talent/deft palm›, ‹talent/fast fingers›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/honeyed words›, ‹talent/lasting poison›, ‹talent/minor magic›, or ‹talent/quick trapsmith›." special="The opportunist can select this edge multiple times."}

::ab[Alchemical Onslaught (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="An opportunist gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls with alchemical weapons and bombs and can add his Intelligence modifier to damage rolls for splash weapons." free="Once per round, he can draw an alchemical weapon." order=pf replace="Weapon training 1"}

pack_mule.name: Pack Mule
description: ## Pack Mule
‹SOURCE Black Markets/26›

Pack mules carry heavy loads with ease and small packages with discretion. Unlike most couriers or porters, pack mules are also trained combatants able to defend their charges should the need arise. Many are willing to transport illegal goods, serving as runners for smugglers or muscle for bandits. Pack mules seeking legitimate work prove useful additions to merchant caravans and adventuring parties.
::ab[Skill Ranks per Level]{icon=skill note="He gains 4 + Int modifier skill ranks every level." alter="The fighter's skill ranks per level"}

::ab[Unobtrusive]{icon=skill note="A pack mule gains ‹skill/Bluff› (Cha), ‹skill/Disguise› (Cha), ‹skill/Sleight of Hand› (Dex), and ‹skill/Stealth› (Dex) as class skills." replace="The bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Efficient Packer (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A pack mule adds a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his fighter level on Sleight of Hand checks to conceal objects on his body. He also adds a bonus equal to 1/2 his class level to his Strength score for the purpose of determining his ‹rule/carrying capacity›." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Weight Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A pack mule learns to be more maneuverable when carrying loads. Whenever he is carrying a medium or heavy load, he reduces the check penalty by 1 (to a minimum of 0) and increases the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by 1." order=pa imp7="The check penalty is now reduced by 2, the max Dex bonus is increased by 2, and the pack mule can now move at his normal speed while carrying a heavy load." imp11="The check penalty is now reduced by 3, the max Dex bonus is increased by 3." imp11="The check penalty is now reduced by 4, the max Dex bonus is increased by 4." ability="A pack mule can move at his normal speed while carrying a medium load." replace="Armor training 1 through 4"}

::ab[Healthy as a Mule (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="Whenever the pack mule takes ‹rule/Constitution, Dexterity, or »Strength damage›, he reduces the amount of ability damage taken by 2, to a minimum of 0." replace="Armor mastery"}

phalanx_soldier.name: Phalanx Soldier
description: ## Phalanx Soldier
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/105›
The phalanx soldier specializes in defensive tactics, using his shield to guard himself and his allies and forming a shield wall like an unbreakable anvil against which his enemies break.

::ab[Stand Firm (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A phalanx soldier gains a +1 bonus to CMD against ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/overrun›, and ‹rule/trip› attempts. This bonus also applies on saves against ‹umr/trample› attacks." increment="The bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}
::ab[Phalanx Fighting (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee ability="When a phalanx soldier wields a shield, he can use any polearm or spear of his size as a one-handed weapon." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Ready Pike (Ex)]{l=5 icon=melee-boost immediate="A phalanx soldier can ‹rule/ready› a weapon with the ‹misc/brace› property, gaining a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls." useInc="4~fighter~5" increment="This bonus~5~4" special="He cannot use this ability when ‹rule/flat-footed›." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Deft Shield (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def-boost passive="The armor check penalty from a shield and the attack roll penalty are reduced by 1 for a phalanx soldier using a ‹eq-armor/tower shield›." imp11="These penalties are reduced by 2." replace="Armor training 2 and 3"}

::ab[Shield Ally (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def-protect move="When a phalanx fighter is using a heavy or tower shield, he can provide partial ‹rule/cover› (+2 cover bonus to AC, +1 bonus on Reflex saves) to himself and all adjacent allies until the beginning of his next turn." imp13="He can instead provide cover (+4 cover bonus to AC, +2 bonus on Reflex saves) and ‹ability/evasion› (as a ‹class/rogue›) to one adjacent ally until the beginning of his next turn. This cover does not allow Stealth checks." imp17="He can provide cover to himself and all adjacent allies, or he can provide improved cover (+8 cover bonus to AC, +4 bonus on Reflex saves, ‹ability/improved evasion›) to a single adjacent ally." replace="Weapon training 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Irresistible Advance (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost passive="A phalanx fighter gains a bonus on bull rush and overrun CMB checks. This bonus depends on the type of shield used: +1 with a buckler, +2 with a light shield, +3 with a heavy shield, or +4 with a tower shield." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Shielded Fortress (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def-protect passive="A phalanx fighter's shield cannot be ‹rule/disarm«ed› or ‹rule/sunder«ed›. He gains *evasion* (as a rogue) when using a shield (*improved evasion* when using a tower shield)." move="A phalanx fighter can provide *evasion* to all adjacent allies until the beginning of his next turn." immediate="He can provide *improved evasion* to an adjacent ally against one attack." order=pmi replace="Weapon mastery"}

polearm_master.name: Polearm Master
description: ## Polearm Master
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/106›
The polearm master is schooled in the ancient wisdom that enemies are best faced at the end of long striking pole, lashing like a serpent before clumsy swords and axes can even be brought to bear.

::ab[Pole Fighting (Ex)]{l=2 icon=melee immediate="A polearm master can shorten the grip on his spear or polearm with reach and use it against adjacent targets. This action results in a -4 penalty on attack rolls with that weapon until he spends another immediate action to return to the normal grip." increment="The penalty on attack rolls~2~4~-3~-1" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Steadfast Pike (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A polearm master gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls with ‹rule/readied› attacks and attacks of opportunity made with a spear or polearm." increment="The bonus~3~4" replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}
::ab[Polearm Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A polearm master gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with spears and polearms." increment="The bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Flexible Flanker (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="A polearm master may choose any square adjacent to him and treat that square as his location for determining who he is ‹rule/flanking›, even if that square is occupied by a creature, object, or solid barrier." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Sweeping Fend (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power ability="A polearm master can use any spear or polearm to make ‹rule/bull rush› maneuvers, though he takes a -4 penalty on combat maneuver checks when making such attempts. When using a spear or polearm to make a ‹rule/trip› maneuver, he treats these weapons as if they had the ‹misc/trip› weapon feature. Weapons with the trip property do not incur this penalty on trip maneuvers." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Step Aside (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def immediate="When a creature threatened by a polearm master takes ‹rule/a 5-foot step› into a square adjacent to him, he can take a 5-foot step. This 5-foot step must be subtracted from his movement on the next turn. He also gains a +2 dodge bonus to his AC against that opponent until the end of his next turn." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Polearm Parry (Ex)]{l=19 icon=protect-def immediate="When an opponent threatened by a polearm master makes a melee attack against an ally, he may grant his ally a +2 shield bonus to AC and DR 5/- against that attack." special="He may use this ability to protect himself, but only if the attacking creature is not adjacent to him." replace="Armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{icon=info note="A polearm master must choose a spear or polearm for *weapon mastery.*" alter="Weapon mastery"}

qadira_dawnflower_dervish.name: Qadira: Dawnflower Dervish
description: ## Qadira: Dawnflower Dervish
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Primer/25›

In Qadira, home of the whirlwind, the ‹monster/giant_>scorpion›, and the ‹monster/djinni›, no enemy is as feared as the dervishes of ‹faith/Sarenrae›. While dervishes can be clerics, paladins, or rangers, zealous fighters join their ranks as well. These spinning warriors prefer light or no armor and wield ‹eq-weapon/scimitar«s› with devastating consequences, moving swiftly over the treacherous desert sands to attack with lightning-fast strikes. They maneuver quickly among their enemies, relying on their speed and their skill to see them through the battle.

::ab[Burst of Speed (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A Dawnflower dervish takes only a -1 penalty to her AC after ‹rule/charging›." imp7="The Dawnflower dervish can charge with no penalty." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Desert Stride (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power passive="A Dawnflower dervish can move through 10 feet of ‹rule/difficult terrain› each round as if it were normal terrain. This benefit stacks with the benefits provided by the ‹feat/Acrobatic Steps› and ‹feat/Nimble Moves› feats." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Rapid Attack (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A Dawnflower dervish can combine a ‹rule/full attack› with a single move. She must forgo the attack at her highest bonus but may take the remaining attacks at any point during her movement. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Lightning Strike (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power ability="As part of a full attack, a Dawnflower dervish can make one additional attack. This attack is at the dervish's highest base attack bonus, but each attack in the round (including the extra one) takes a -2 penalty." replace="Armor training 4"}

@HL[Suggested Feats:] ‹feat/Agile Maneuvers›, ‹feat/Combat Expertise›, ‹feat/Dazzling Display›, ‹feat/Dervish Dance›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Spring Attack›, ‹feat/Step Up›, ‹feat/Weapon Finesse›, ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (scimitar), ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› (scimitar), ‹feat/Whirlwind Attack›.

relic_master.name: Relic Master
description: ## Relic Master
‹SOURCE Weapon Master's Handbook/27›
Commonly trained in the well-funded temples of Osirion or Qadira, the relic master is skilled in magic item mastery.
::cskill{gain=ka~umd lose=ha~ride}

::ab[Improved Item Mastery (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A relic master can use an ‹main/feats_>item mastery« feat› she knows with a magic item that doesn't have an appropriate spell in its construction requirements. The magic item must meet all other requirements of the item mastery feat. Alternatively, if she is using a magic item with an appropriate spell in its construction requirements, she can use an item mastery feat without it counting against the feat's total uses per day." useInc="4~fighter~3" replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Relic Channeler (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="A relic master can increase the potency of a magic item she wields by investing it with a bit of her vitality. If the item is armor, a shield, or a weapon, its enhancement bonus increases by 1 to a maximum of +5. If the magic item creates a spell or has a save DC, its effective caster level increases by 1." useMod=Con1 useNC=1 useUnit=minute useM=1 replace="Weapon training"}

::ab[Improvised Item Mastery (Su)]{l=19 icon=power fullround="The relic master can select one item mastery feat whose prerequisites she meets but that she doesn't already have. She gains access to this feat, though she can't use it as a prerequisite for other feats or options. If she selects a different item mastery feat, she loses access to her previous use of *improvised item mastery* and any magic effect created with it ends immediately. Any daily uses of a selected *improvised item mastery* feat count against all *improvised mastery feats* selected in the same day." replace="Armor mastery"}

roughrider.name: Roughrider
description: ## Roughrider
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/106›
Roughriders study and practice the fine points of mounted combat, drilling endlessly with warbeasts - from noble thoroughbreds to trained monsters - to form a perfect synergy between rider and steed.

::ab[Steadfast Mount (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="After a roughrider has spent 1 hour practicing with a mount, the mount gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC and a +1 morale bonus on saves, but only while the roughrider is mounted on it or adjacent to it." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Armored Charger (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A roughrider no longer suffers armor check penalties on Ride skill checks. His mount's speed is not reduced when carrying a medium load or wearing medium barding." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Mounted Mettle (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A roughrider and his mount gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when he is mounted or adjacent to his mount." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Leap from the Saddle (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="After a roughrider's mount takes a single move, he may attempt a fast dismount (DC 20 Ride check). If he succeeds, he can take a ‹rule/full attack› action." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Relentless Steed (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power-roll passive="A roughrider's mount does not reduce its speed when wearing heavy barding or carrying a heavy load." ability="The roughrider may reroll a Ride skill check or a saving throw made by the mount, but must use the second roll even if it is worse." useInc="4~fighter~11" order=pau replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Ride Them Down (Ex)]{l=15 icon=melee ability="A roughrider can spur his mount on while readying an attack. If a roughrider's mount takes a single move, the roughrider can make a full attack, taking his attacks at any point during his mount's movement. If he has the ‹feat/Trample› feat, he may substitute an ‹rule/overrun› combat maneuver for each of his attacks. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity against the roughrider but not his mount." replace="Armor training 4"}
::ab[Unavoidable Onslaught (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power passive="A roughrider's mounted charge is not blocked by friendly creatures or ‹rule/difficult terrain›." replace="Armor training 4"}
::ab[Indomitable Steed (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A roughrider and his steed gain DR 5/- when mounted." replace="Armor mastery"}

savage_warrior.name: Savage Warrior
description: ## Savage Warrior
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/107›

Warriors' might is not measured only by their skill with steel, but also by their ability to inflict death with fang and claw, horn and hoof, and every exotic appendage the natural and unnatural world has to offer.
::ab[Spark of Life (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A savage warrior gains a +1 bonus on saving throws made against energy drain and death effects." increment="The bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Natural Savagery (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A savage warrior gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with natural weapons. This bonus also applies to CMB and CMD for ‹rule/grappl«ing<e›." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Savage Charge (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost-down passive="When a savage warrior attacks with a natural weapon at the end of a ‹rule/charge›, he gains a bonus on his natural weapon attack rolls equal to ‹misc/half› his fighter level, while suffering a penalty to his AC equal to ‹misc/half› his fighter level. These replace the normal attack roll bonus and AC penalty for charging. This bonus also applies to his CMB for a ‹rule/bull rush› or ‹rule/overrun› combat maneuvers made when charging." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Careful Claw (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="When using a natural weapon to attack a creature using ‹spell/fire shield› or a similar effect that damages creatures attacking it (such as a ‹monster/barbed devil«'s› barbed defense), a savage warrior reduces the damage from such effects by an amount equal to 1/2 his fighter level." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Greater Savage Charge (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def-melee passive="When using *savage charge,* the AC penalty is reduced to 1/4 his fighter level instead of 1/2 his fighter level. In addition, a savage warrior can charge through friendly creatures and ‹rule/difficult terrain›." replace="Weapon training 4"}
::ab[Natural Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=info note="A savage warrior must choose one natural weapon." replace="Weapon mastery"}

scrapper.name: Scrapper
description: ## Scrapper
‹SOURCE Construct Handbook/14›
Scrappers are adept at collecting the leftovers from their enemy's armor and using these scraps to improve their own armor.

::ab[Scrap Armor (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def flavor="A scrapper learns to salvage scrap from constructs or other pieces of armor and attach them to his own armor." ability="Salvaging scrap from a destroyed construct or from another creature's armor takes 1 minute. Scrap from a construct grants the scrapper a natural armor bonus to AC equal to one-quarter the construct's Hit Dice (minimum +1). If the scrap is salvaged from a piece of armor, it instead grants a natural armor bonus to AC equal to 1 + the armor's enhancement bonus (if any). The salvaged armor piece is flimsy and prone to breaking. Scrap is immediately destroyed by any ‹rule/sunder› combat maneuver that targets the armor to which the scrap is attached. Otherwise, scrap crumbles and falls away after a number of days equal to the natural armor bonus that it grants." imp11="Scrap salvaged from a construct also grants DR/adamantine equal to one-quarter of the construct's Hit Dice." replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Scrapper Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A scrapper selects one fighter ‹ability/weapon group<s›. He gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when performing a sunder combat maneuver with a weapon from the chosen group. He does not provoke attacks of opportunity when performing a sunder combat maneuver with a weapon from the chosen group. If the scrapper has the ‹feat/Improved Sunder› feat, the bonuses on attack and damage rolls from this ability double." repeat="A scrapper can either take the fighter's *weapon training* class feature or improve his bonuses from the *scrapper training* class feature by 1.~13~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}

seasoned_commander.name: Seasoned Commander
description: ## Seasoned Commander
‹SOURCE Villain Codex/164›
The seasoned commander excels at leading troops through inspiration and the use of unit tactics.

::ab[Strategic Training (Ex)]{icon=skill note="A seasoned commander gains 4 skill ranks + a number of skill ranks equal to his Intelligence modifier at each level instead of the normal 2 skill ranks + Intelligence modifier at each level. A seasoned commander adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA), ‹skill/Knowledge (geography)› (INT), ‹skill/Knowledge (nobility)› (INT), ‹skill/Linguistics› (INT), and ‹skill/Sense Motive› (WIS) to his list of class skills." alter="Skill ranks per level~class skills" replace="Bonus fighter combat feat gained at 1st level~proficiency with heavy armor and tower shields"}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{l=3 head icon=power ability="A seasoned commander gains the ‹class/cavalier«'s› *tactician* class feature, treating his fighter level as his cavalier level for the purposes of this ability." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Inspiring Speech (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A seasoned commander can give an inspiring speech once per day. If he does, all allies who can hear his speech gain the benefits of ‹performance/inspire courage›, granting bonuses 1 lower than those granted by a bard of the seasoned commander's fighter level, lasting 1 round per fighter level + his Charisma modifier (abilities that affect a bardic performance, such as ‹feat/Lingering Performance›, have no effect on this ability)." imp7="He can perform the speech as a @HLmove_action." imp9="He can use this ability twice a day." imp13="He can perform the speech as a @HLswift_action." imp15="He can use this ability three times a day." replace="Weapon training 1 and 3"}

::ab[Inspire Greatness (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost ability="A seasoned commander can also choose to use his *inspiring speech* to grant the effects of ‹performance/inspire greatness› to one ally (instead of *inspire courage*)." imp11="He can target two allies while using this ability." imp13="He can target up to three allies with this ability." imp15="He can now target up to four allies." imp17="He can target up to five allies." imp19="He can target up to six allies." special="This ability functions in all other respects like the seasoned commander's *inspire courage* ability. Whichever *inspiring speech* he chooses to use, if the seasoned commander uses an *inspiring speech* before the duration of his previous *inspiring speech* ends, the new *inspiring speech* replaces the old *speech.*" replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Greater Tactician (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="The seasoned commander gains the ‹class/cavalier«'s› *greater tactician* class feature, treating his fighter level as his cavalier level for the purposes of this ability." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Inspire Heroics (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost ability="A seasoned commander can choose to use his *inspiring speech* to grant the effects of ‹performance/inspire heroics› to one ally (instead of *inspire courage* or *inspire greatness*)." imp17="He can target up to two allies with this ability." imp19="He can target up to three allies with this ability." special="This ability functions in all other respects like the seasoned commander's *inspire courage* ability." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Master Tactician (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="The seasoned commander gains the cavalier's *master tactician* class feature, treating his fighter level as his cavalier level for the purposes of this ability." replace="Weapon training 4"}

sensate.name: Sensate
description: ## Sensate
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/118›

A sensate perceives battle through senses beyond mortal ken, anticipating his opponents' movements before they even begin to act. As long as he keeps his focus about him, he fights with a skill rivaling that of the finest masters-at-arms.
::cskill{gain=acro~per~sm lose=ha~intm~kd~ke}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Sensates are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, light armor, medium armor, and shields (except tower shields)." replace="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Guarded Senses (Su)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A sensate gains a +1 bonus on saves against sonic effects, figments, glamers, patterns, ‹umr/gaze› attacks, and scent-based attacks." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}
::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Su)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A sensate gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge› as the ‹class/barbarian› ability of the same name, except supernatural in nature." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Centered Senses (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost-def move="A sensate can center his senses. While centered, a sensate gains a +1 insight bonus on attack rolls, weapon damage rolls, and Will saves." increment="This bonus~5~4" special="A sensate loses his centered state if he gains the confused, dazed, fascinated, staggered, or stunned condition; falls unconscious; or fails a Will save." replace="Weapon training 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Improved Uncanny Dodge (Su)]{l=7 icon=def passive="A sensate gains ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› as the barbarian ability of the same name, except supernatural in nature." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Evasion (Su)]{l=11 icon=def passive="A sensate gains ‹ability/evasion› while using centered senses. This functions as the rogue ability of the same name, except that it is supernatural in nature and works regardless of the sensate's armor and encumbrance." replace="Armor training 3"}
::ab[Steady (Su)]{l=15 icon=boost swift="A sensate can center himself as a @HLswift_action." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Perfect Senses (Su)]{l=19 icon=power passive="A sensate's senses reach perfection, and he can easily notice illusions and unseen foes by combining his senses. While centered, he gains the effects of ‹spell/true seeing› as well as ‹umr/blindsense› 60 feet. An unseen creature that makes no sound and leaves no scent (such as a creature under the effects of ‹spell/invisibility›, ‹spell/silence›, and ‹spell/negate aroma›) can still avoid both the *true seeing* and the *blindsense.*" replace="Armor mastery"}

::ab[Precision (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost-def-roll passive="A sensate rolls twice to confirm critical hits while centered, taking the more favorable result. Opponents must roll twice to confirm critical hits against him, taking the less favorable result. The latter ability does not function when the sensate is denied his Dexterity bonus." replace="Weapon mastery"}

shielded_fighter.name: Shielded Fighter
description: ## Shielded Fighter
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/108›

A shielded fighter focuses on both offense and defense, blending weapon and shield in perfect balance to impede his enemies while delivering deadly blows, and even turning the shield itself into a formidable weapon. These fighting school benefits apply when wielding a weapon and a shield simultaneously.

::ab[Active Defense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A shielded fighter gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC when wielding a shield and ‹misc/fighting defensively›, using ‹feat/Combat Expertise›, or using ‹misc/total defense›." increment="The dodge bonus~3~4" swift="He may share this bonus with one adjacent ally, or ‹misc/half› of the bonus (minimum +0) with all adjacent allies, until the beginning of his next turn." order=pw replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Shield Fighter (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-def passive="A shielded fighter gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when making a ‹misc/shield bash›." increment="The bonus~5~4" special="With a ‹rule/full attack› action, a shielded fighter may alternate between using his weapon or his shield for each attack. This action does not grant additional attacks or incur penalties as two-weapon fighting does." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Shield Buffet (Ex)]{l=9 icon=lower move="A shielded fighter may make a combat maneuver check to use his shield to impede an adjacent enemy. If successful, the target suffers a -2 penalty on its attack rolls against the shielded fighter and a -2 penalty to AC on attacks made by the shielded fighter until the beginning of his next turn." imp13="A shielded fighter may use this ability as a @HLswift_action." replace="Weapon training 2 and 3"}

::ab[Shield Guard (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def swift="A shielded fighter may designate one square adjacent to him. He may designate two squares if using a heavy shield or three squares if using a tower shield, but these squares must be contiguous. Enemies in these squares cannot ‹rule/flank› the shielded fighter and do not count for flanking with other creatures. This effect lasts until he moves from his position or uses another swift action to change the affected squares." replace="Weapon training 4"}
::ab[Shield Mastery (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A shielded fighter gains DR 5/- when wielding a shield." replace="Armor mastery"}

::ab[Shield Ward (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="A shielded fighter gains ‹ability/evasion› (as a ‹class/rogue›) while wielding a shield, and adds his shield bonus to his AC (not including enhancement bonuses) on Reflex saves and to his touch AC. In addition, his shield cannot be ‹rule/disarm«ed› or ‹rule/sunder«ed›." replace="Weapon mastery"}

siegebreaker.name: Siegebreaker
description: ## Siegebreaker
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/11›
The siegebreaker is trained to break through lines of enemy soldiers.

::ab[Breaker Rush (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def-boost ability="A siegebreaker can attempt ‹rule/bull rush› or ‹rule/overrun› combat maneuvers without provoking attacks of opportunity. When he performs either combat maneuver, he deals an amount of bludgeoning damage equal to his Strength bonus (minimum 1). If he has ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush› or ‹feat/Improved Overrun›, the damage dealt by the appropriate maneuver increases by 2 and he adds any enhancement bonus from his armor or shield (though such enhancement bonuses do not stack, if both armor and shield are magic)." replace="The feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Armored Vigor (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def swift="A siegebreaker can gain 2 ‹rule/temporary hit points› that last for 1 minute." useMod=Con3 special="He can use this ability only while wearing armor." incrementPlain="The amount of temporary hit points~2~6~4~2" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Breaker Momentum (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power free="When a siegebreaker successfully bull rushes a foe, he can attempt an overrun combat maneuver check against that foe." replace="The feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Persistent Menace (Ex)]{l=4 icon=info choice="A siegebreaker must choose either ‹feat/Step Up› or ‹feat/Disruptive› as his bonus feat, but need not meet the prerequisites." alter="The feat gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Disorienting Blow (Ex)]{l=8 icon=lower immediate="A siegebreaker can distract a foe he just hit with an attack or combat maneuver, imposing a -2 penalty on the foe's attack rolls, caster level checks, or skill checks for 1 round. The foe can negate this penalty with a successful Fortitude save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the siegebreaker's fighter level + his Strength modifier)." imp14="The penalty increases to -4." replace="The bonus feat gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Masterful Distraction (Ex)]{l=20 icon=lower ability="A siegebreaker can ‹misc/nauseate<d› a foe he targets with his *disorienting blow* instead of inflicting a penalty." replace="Weapon mastery"}

skirmisher.name: Skirmisher
description: ## Skirmisher
**Source** ‹source/Ultimate Wilderness« pg. 50›, [errata](https://paizo.com/paizo/faq/v5748nruor1hj#v5748eaic9wc9)

Not all battles are fought between armies; sometimes, a settlement or nation faces a threat too dire to fight in the open. In order to repel a much larger enemy force, some soldiers must abandon equipment and tactics designed for open-field warfare and instead rely on ambushes, hit-and-run tactics, and sabotage to whittle down their enemies' strength and morale. Skirmishers use harsh weather and unforgiving terrain to their advantage, attacking isolated groups of enemies that cannot flee or fight back effectively. Many tribal warriors also employ small-unit tactics because it is part of their warrior culture, because they cannot field a large army, or simply because they refuse to fight on the enemy's terms.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A skirmisher is proficient with light armor and shields but not with medium armor, heavy armor, or tower shields." replace="The fighter's armor proficiency"}

::ab[Wilderness Training (Ex)]{icon=skill note="A skirmisher relies on his skills and the element of surprise rather than the heaviest shields and armor. He gains ‹skill/Acrobatics› (DEX) and ‹skill/Stealth› (DEX) as class skills. In addition, the skirmisher gains a number of skill ranks at each level equal to 4 + his Intelligence modifier, instead of 2 + his Intelligence modifier." alter="Class skills~skill ranks per level"}

::ab[Conditioning (Ex)]{head l=2 icon=def choice="A skirmisher becomes more resistant to mental and physical threats that are commonly employed by his enemies or often encountered in the terrain in which he operates. The skirmisher must choose one of the specializations below, gaining a +1 bonus on saving throws of the appropriate type." increment="The saving throw bonus~2~4" replace="2nd level fighter bonus feat"}

::ab[Alpine Training (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="A skirmisher operating in mountainous terrain becomes resistant to cold climates and the rigors of long treks at high altitudes. He gains a bonus on Fortitude saving throws against ‹rule/altitude sickness›, exposure to ‹rule/cold« weather›, and other effects that cause ‹misc/fatigue› or ‹misc/exhaustion›."}

::ab[Counter-Interrogation Training (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="A skirmisher undertaking infiltration missions learns to resist interrogation and persuasion. The skirmisher gains a bonus on Will saving throws against charms and divinations."}

::ab[Jungle Training (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="Through repeated exposure, a skirmisher operating in jungles and wetlands builds up a tolerance against pathogens and toxins. He gains a bonus on Fortitude saving throws against disease and poison."}

::ab[Light Infantry Training (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="A skirmisher fighting in a light infantry unit learns to avoid fire from enemy artillery, siege weapons, and spellcasters. He gains a bonus on Reflex saving throws against attacks that deal half damage on a successful save (such as the ‹spell/fireball› spell) but not against traps, natural hazards, or environmental effects."}

::ab[Reconnaissance Training (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A skirmisher operating in heavily trapped or naturally hazardous areas learns to expertly avoid them." icon=def passive="He gains a +1 bonus on Reflex saving throws to avoid traps, natural hazards, and environmental effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Mobility Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def-boost passive="A skirmisher learns to be more maneuverable while wearing light or no armor. He gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC while wearing light or no armor and while carrying no more than a light load. Anything that causes the skirmisher to lose his Dexterity bonus to AC also causes the skirmisher to lose this dodge bonus. In addition, a skirmisher gains an enhancement bonus of +5 feet to his base speed. This benefit applies only when he is wearing no armor or light armor, and when he is carrying no more than a light load." imp7="The bonus to speed becomes +10 feet, and the dodge bonus becomes +2." imp11="The dodge bonus becomes +3." imp15="The dodge bonus increases to +4." replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Mobile Mastery (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A skirmisher learns to position his body and conceal his body proportions with clothes so well that it is difficult to hit him. He gains a 20% miss chance on attacks against him. Mobile mastery functions even when the skirmisher is ‹rule/flat-footed›, but not when he is immobilized or ‹misc/helpless›, when he wears medium or heavy armor, or when he carries a medium or heavy load." replace="Armor mastery"}

spear_fighter.name: Spear Fighter
description: ## Spear Fighter
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/11›

The spear is one of the oldest weapons known to most humanoid races, and no weapon has seen as much use across the different continents of Golarion. One of the oldest spear-fighting styles originates in Vudra, where many martial arts traditions consider the spear to be the ultimate weapon.
::cskill{gain=acro lose=ride}

::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="A spear fighter is not proficient with heavy armor or tower shields." order=na ability="He gains ‹feat/Dodge› as a bonus feat, even if he doesn't fulfill the prerequisites, but he can't use this feat while wearing heavy armor or using a shield." alter="The fighter's armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Balanced Stride (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="The spear fighter gains a +1 bonus on Acrobatics checks to avoid attacks of opportunity." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Spear Parry (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def-down ability="A spear fighter learns to parry his opponents' attacks with his spear. This functions as a swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/opportune parry and riposte› deed except as noted below. The spear fighter must use a weapon from the spears ‹ability/weapon group<s› to perform the parry. He doesn't need panache to parry an attack or to make an attack against a creature whose attack he has parried; instead, he takes a cumulative -4 penalty on all attacks (including further parry attempts) until the end of his next turn." imp7="The penalty becomes -3." imp11="The penalty is reduced to -2." imp17="The penalty becomes -1." imp19="A spear fighter can use *spear parry* without penalty." replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4~armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=info choice="A spear fighter must choose the spears weapon group for the *weapon training* class feature." alter="Weapon training"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=info choice="A spear fighter must choose a weapon from the spears weapon group for his *weapon mastery* class feature." alter="Weapon mastery"}

steelbound_fighter.name: Steelbound Fighter
description: ## Steelbound Fighter
‹SOURCE Haunted Heroes Handbook/22›

Whenever a particularly imposing intelligent weapon controls the mind of its wielder for long enough, spiritual echoes of this dominance can have strange resonances throughout that wielder's bloodline for generations to come. The steelbound fighter is sometimes the result of such a legacy: a fighter who has impressive martial resolve and technique with a specific weapon as the result of a powerful relationship a similar weapon had with one of his ancestors.

::ab[Steelbound Weapon (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down choice="A steelbound fighter selects one type of weapon (such as ‹eq-weapon/short sword«s›, ‹eq-weapon/longbow«s›, or ‹eq-weapon/heavy flail«s›). He gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› with that specific weapon. Whenever he isn't wielding or carrying at least one weapon of this type (the weapon can be broken but not destroyed), a steelbound fighter takes a -2 penalty to his Wisdom score and can't benefit from his fighter class abilities (excluding bonus feats gained from fighter levels). Once the steelbound fighter's weapon awakens (see *steelbound awakening* below), these penalties apply whenever the fighter isn't carrying or wielding that particular weapon." replace="The fighter's 1st-level bonus feat"}

:::ab{l=5 icon=power action=Ability title="Steelbound Awakening (Su)" incrementAt="The steelbound weapon's bonus~9~13~17~20~2" imp9="Its ability scores increase to 12 and it gains the ability to speak in its wielder's native tongue. The weapon gains the ability to cast a 3rd-level spell once per day (the choice of the spell is made by the steelbound fighter, but is subject to GM approval, and once chosen, it can't be changed), with a caster level equal to the steelbound fighter's level. The spell's save DC is based off of the item's Charisma." imp13="Its ability scores increase to 14 and its senses extend to 120 feet. The weapon gains a ‹rule/special purpose› of the steelbound fighter's choice (subject to GM approval)." imp17="Its ability scores increase to 16 and it gains telepathy as well as darkvision with a range of 60 feet. The weapon can now use its spell-like ability three times a day, and can select a second 3rd-level spell that it can use once per day." special="A steelbound fighter transfers the effects of his *steelbound awakening* to a new weapon of the proper type automatically after carrying that weapon on his person for 24 hours, but can have only one weapon under the effects of *steelbound awakening* at any given time." replace="Weapon training"}

One nonintelligent weapon carried by the steelbound fighter becomes possessed by the latent spiritual energies that cling to his soul, transforming that weapon into an ‹rule/intelligent «weapon<items›. The weapon must be of the type the fighter chose at 1st level as his steelbound weapon. The steelbound weapon grants its wielder ‹feat/Alertness› as a bonus feat as long as the weapon is wielded. A steelbound fighter gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls with his steelbound weapon.

A steelbound weapon gains Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores of 10 when it awakens, and has the same alignment as its wielder. It communicates via empathy, and has senses that extend 30 feet. It has 5 ranks in a skill of the steelbound fighter's choice.

swarm_fighter.name: Swarm Fighter
description: ## Swarm Fighter
‹SOURCE Kobolds of Golarion/23›

Kobolds know there's strength in numbers, and they rarely send fewer than two kobolds out on patrol. This reliance on one another extends to combat tactics too - overwhelming numbers and tight quarters are the ‹race/kobold› fighter's friends. Scuttling between the legs of friend and foe alike, the swarm fighter is an unshakable combatant.
::prereq{r=Kobold}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A swarm fighter is proficient with all simple and martial weapons. She is proficient with light and medium armor, but not with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Athletic Prowess (Ex)]{icon=skill-boost note="A swarm fighter adds ‹skill/Acrobatics› (DEX) and ‹skill/Climb› (STR) to her class skills, and gains a bonus on checks with these skills equal to ‹misc/half› her swarm fighter level."}

::ab[Mobility (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The swarm fighter gains ‹feat/Mobility› as a bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites." replace="The bonus feat a fighter gains at first level" special="This feat can't be replaced at a later level."}

::ab[Safety in Numbers (Ex)]{icon=def flavor="A swarm fighter benefits greatly from the proximity of her allies." passive="She gains a morale bonus on saves against fear effects equal to the number of allies within 20 feet of her. This bonus can't exceed half her level." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Bonus Teamwork Feats (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="The swarm fighter gains a bonus ‹main/teamwork feat›." incrementOrd=She~2~4 incrementDesc="gains a" incrementEnd=" teamwork feat." replace="The bonus combat feats the fighter normally receives at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels" special="She gains the other bonus combat feats as normal."}

::ab[Share Space (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-def ability="The swarm fighter can move into the space of another creature at least one size category larger than she is. She may end her movement in, and make attacks from, that space. She must succeed at the Acrobatics check for moving through an occupied square to move into a space occupied by an enemy. While sharing the space of another creature, she gains a +2 shield bonus to AC and a +1 circumstance bonus on Reflex saves." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Strike the Underbelly (Ex)]{l=9 icon=lower passive="Creatures with which the swarm fighter shares a space are denied their Dexterity bonus to Armor Class against the swam fighter's attacks." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Always Underfoot (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power ability="The swarm fighter gains ‹feat/Step Up› as a bonus feat. A creature sharing a square with the swarm fighter is considered adjacent for the purposes of this feat." imp17="The swarm fighter gains ‹feat/Following Step› as a bonus feat." replace="Weapon training 3 and 4"}

::ab[Soft Underbelly (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="Any attacks made against creatures with which the swarm fighter shares a space automatically confirm all critical threats." replace="Weapon mastery"}

tactician.name: Tactician
description: ## Tactician
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/47›

While many fighters focus on the fundamentals of melee and ranged combat, there are those who are trained to view the bigger picture on the battlefield. These fighters use their training and tactical acumen to overcome challenges that would overwhelm mere brute strength and skill at arms.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A tactician is not proficient with heavy armor or tower shields." alter="Armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Strategic Training (Ex)]{icon=skill note="A tactician gains 4 skill points + a number of skill points equal to his Intelligence modifier at each level, instead of the normal 2 skill points + Intelligence modifier at each level. Furthermore, ‹skill/Diplomacy› (Cha), ‹skill/Knowledge (geography)› (Int), ‹skill/Knowledge (nobility)› (Int), ‹skill/Linguistics› (Int), and ‹skill/Sense Motive› (Wis) are all class skills for the tactician." replace="The bonus fighter combat feat gained at 1st level"}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power note="A tactician may choose ‹feat/Skill Focus› or any ‹main/teamwork feat›, in addition to combat feats, as bonus feats."}
::ab[Tactical Awareness (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A tactician gains a +1 bonus on initiative checks." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A tactician gains this ability as the ‹class/cavalier› class feature." useL=fighter useUnit=time special="If the tactician also has cavalier levels, these levels stack for determining the number of uses per day, and he can take the better progression." order=au replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Cooperative Combatant (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="When a tactician uses the ‹rule/aid another<_2› special attack, he may affect one additional ally per point of Intelligence bonus. For each ally that a tactician aids, he can pick whether to grant that ally the +2 bonus on its next attack against the opponent or the +2 bonus to AC against the opponent's next attack on that ally, and can grant different allies different bonuses." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Battle Insight (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost swift="A tactician can grant his Intelligence modifier as an insight bonus on the attack rolls made by a single ally within line of sight that can both see and hear the tactician. That ally gains the bonus until the end of the tactician's next turn." useMod=Int3 replace="Armor training 4"}

taldor_rondelero_duelist.name: Taldor: Rondelero Duelist
description: ## Taldor: Rondelero Duelist
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Primer/25›

Taldor's warriors have perfected the art of fighting with the ‹eq-weapon/falcata› and ‹eq-armor/buckler› in an art they call rondelero. They use their bucklers both to defend themselves and to launch unexpected strikes while chopping and hewing at their foes' unprotected bodies and heads with their falcatas. Rondeleros wear any type of armor, but favor bucklers, and are comfortable using both hands for offense and defense.

::ab[Buckler Bash (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A rondelero can perform a ‹misc/shield bash› with a buckler (use the same damage and critical modifier as for a ‹eq-armor/light shield›)." replace=Bravery}

::ab[Buckler Catch (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee-down ability="A rondelero can catch his opponent's weapon between his buckler and his forearm, effectively wedging the hafts of polearms and hammers or the flats of blades. This functions as a ‹rule/disarm› combat maneuver, and the rondelero gains a +4 bonus on the roll. If the rondelero's attack fails by 10 or more, he suffers a -2 penalty to his AC until the start of his next turn." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Strong Swing (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-melee order=pa passive="A rondelero gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when wielding a falcata and buckler that applies to attacks made by either hand." increment="p!These bonuses~5~4" ability="With a ‹rule/full-attack› action, a rondelero may alternate between using his falcata or his buckler for each attack. This does not grant additional attacks or incur penalties as two-weapon fighting does." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A rondelero gains *armor training 1,* and can move at his normal speed in medium armor." imp15="The rondelero gains *armor training 2,* and can move at his normal speed in heavy armor." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Chopping Blow (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee standard="A rondelero can make a single melee attack with a falcata. If the attack hits, he may make a ‹rule/sunder› combat maneuver against the target of his attack as a @HLfree_action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity." replace="Armor training 3"}

@HL[Suggested Feats:] ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (falcata), ‹feat/Improved Shield Bash›, ‹feat/Improved Two-Weapon Fighting›, ‹feat/Power Attack›, ‹feat/Shield Slam›, ‹feat/Shield Master›, ‹feat/Taldan Duelist›, ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting›, ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (falcata), ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› (falcata).

thunderstriker.name: Thunderstriker
description: ## Thunderstriker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/48›

The thunderstriker adopts an unusual fighting style, gripping a heavy weapon with both hands and switching to a defensive posture with weapon and buckler, lashing out with the shield with surprising speed and power.
::ab[Strapped Shield (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A thunderstriker takes no penalty on attack rolls when using a weapon in two hands while wearing a ‹eq-armor/buckler›." replace="Armor training 1"}
::ab[Hardbuckler (Ex)]{l=7 icon=melee ability="A thunderstriker may make ‹misc/shield bash› attacks with a buckler as if it were a ‹eq-armor/light shield›." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Knockback Smash (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost passive="When a thunderstriker uses his buckler to attack, he gains a bonus equal to the enhancement bonus of the buckler on both attack and damage rolls." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Hammer and Anvil (Ex)]{l=13 icon=boost passive="A thunderstriker suffers only half the normal penalties for two-weapon fighting when using a buckler as his off-hand weapon." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Buckler Defense (Ex)]{l=15 icon=def passive="A thunderstriker retains partial use of his buckler even when using a weapon in both hands or in each hand (rather than losing his shield bonus until the beginning of his next turn). He gains a +1 shield bonus to AC and may apply the benefits of any feats he knows that require the use of a shield, but does not benefit from any magical properties his shield may possess." replace="Armor training 4"}
::ab[Balanced Bashing (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost passive="A thunderstriker no longer suffers two-weapon fighting penalties when using a buckler as his off-hand weapon." replace="Weapon training 4"}
::ab[Improved Buckler Defense (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A thunderstriker does not forfeit his shield bonus to AC from a buckler when fighting two-handed." replace="Armor mastery"}

titan_fighter.name: Titan Fighter
description: ## Titan Fighter
‹SOURCE Giant Hunter's Handbook/21›
Titan fighters make use of enormous weapons others can barely lift. Titan fighters have the following class features.

::ab[Giant Weapon Wielder (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A titan fighter can wield two-handed melee weapons intended for creatures one size category larger than himself, treating them as two-handed weapons. He takes an additional -2 penalty on attack rolls when using an oversized two-handed weapon." replace="The fighter's 1st level bonus feat"}

::ab[Incredible Heft (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A titan fighter becomes more skilled at wielding weapons intended for creatures one size category larger than himself. The penalty on attack rolls for using such weapons is reduced by 1, including when using oversized two-handed weapons." incrementDesc="is now reduced by" incrementPlain="This penalty~3~4~2" incrementEnd=" (minimum 0)." replace="Armor training"}

::ab[Unstoppable Momentum (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-def-power passive="A titan fighter gains a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks and to CMD while wielding a weapon sized for a creature of a larger size category." increment="The bonus~5~4" order=ap ability="When wielding oversized weapons, the titan fighter can attempt to ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/drag›, ‹rule/overrun›, ‹rule/reposition›, and ‹rule/trip› creatures up to two sizes categories larger than himself." replace="Weapon training"}

tower_shield_specialist.name: Tower Shield Specialist
description: ## Tower Shield Specialist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/48›

Many fighters believe the ‹eq-armor/tower shield› is a tool suitable only for troops on the battlefield, claiming it is too large and bulky to use in skirmishes or within dungeon corridors. Tower shield specialists defy those notions, using their massive shields with startling skill and incredible effect. They use these seemingly clumsy shields to perform deft maneuvers that confound their enemies.

::ab[Burst Barrier (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A tower shield specialist can use his shield to screen himself from burst spells and effects, gaining a +1 bonus on Reflex saves against them while employing a tower shield." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Tower Shield Training (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A tower shield specialist gains *armor training* as normal, but while he employs a tower shield, the armor penalty is reduced by 3 and the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by his armor increases by 2. The benefit increases every four levels thereafter as per standard *armor training;* if the tower shield specialist is not employing a tower shield, the benefits to *armor training* revert to the normal bonuses."}

::ab[Tower Shield Specialist (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="When a tower shield specialist employs a tower shield in combat, he does not take the -2 penalty on attack rolls because of the shield's encumbrance." replace="Weapon training 1"}
::ab[Tower Shield Defense (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="While using a tower shield, a tower shield specialist gains his shield bonus against touch attacks." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Immediate Repositioning (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power immediate="A tower shield specialist can reposition his tower shield to another facing, but he cannot use this ability to interrupt an attack." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Tower Shield Evasion (Ex)]{l=16 icon=def ability="While using a tower shield, the tower shield specialist gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the ‹class/rogue› class ability." imp20="The shield specialist gains ‹talent/improved evasion›, as the rogue advanced talent, while using a tower shield." replace="Weapon training 4~weapon mastery"}

trench_fighter.name: Trench Fighter
description: ## Trench Fighter
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #71: Rasputin Must Die!/67›
Advances in technology have made archaic armors obsolete by the twentieth century, and modern soldiers concentrate training on firearms and swift feet.

::ab[Trench Warfare (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def order=pc choice="A trench fighter can select one specific type of firearm (such as a machine gun, revolver, or rifle). He gains a bonus equal to his Dexterity modifier on damage rolls when firing that type of firearm." imp7="The trench fighter picks up another type of firearm, gaining these bonuses for those types as well." imp11="The trench fighter picks up a third type of firearm, gaining these bonuses for those types as well." imp15="The trench fighter picks up a fourth type of firearm, gaining these bonuses for those types as well." passive="When behind partial, normal, or improved ‹rule/cover›, a trench fighter gains an additional +2 AC bonus from the cover." replace="Armor training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

tribal_fighter.name: Tribal Fighter
description: ## Tribal Fighter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/50›

A tribal fighter knows that it is not the weapon that matters but the hand that wields it. Instead of encasing himself in metal armor like the soldiers fighting and dying for the so-called civilized lands do, he prefers to wear something he or his ancestors have killed. These relics and trophies help the tribal fighter attain a battle focus that allows him to perform incredible feats of violence with the simplest of weapons.

::ab[Forbidden Armor]{icon=down note="A tribal fighter favors traditional armor and shields, avoiding other types out of spiritual devotion, superstitious suspicion, or respect for tradition. He can attain his *battle focus* only while using armaments of the appropriate types. Like a ‹class/druid›, a tribal fighter is prohibited from wearing metal armor and using metal shields. A tribal fighter who wears prohibited armor or uses a prohibited shield loses the benefits of the *battle focus* and *tribal weapon training* abilities while doing so and for 24 hours after removing the prohibited armor or shield." alter="Armor proficiency"}

::ab[Battle Focus (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A tribal fighter learns to fight with his bare hands and wield traditional tribal weapons so effortlessly that they are almost extensions of his arms. He gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. For the purpose of meeting the prerequisites of ‹main/style feat«s› and feats that have style feats as prerequisites, the tribal fighter counts as having a number of skill ranks equal to his fighter level in all skills in which he is trained." replace="The fighter feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Tribal Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost ability="When a tribal fighter gains the *weapon training* ability, he must choose the tribal ‹ability/weapon group<s› and doesn't later gain additional weapon groups, though the *weapon training* bonus for tribal weapons still improves at 9th level and every 4 levels thereafter. Additionally, if the tribal fighter has combat feats that apply to a specific weapon type (such as ‹feat/Improved Critical› or ‹feat/Weapon Focus›) and he has selected a weapon that belongs to the tribal weapon group, he can apply the benefits of that feat to all weapons that belong to the tribal weapon group that would legally apply (for instance, a feat that applies only to throwing weapons wouldn't apply to ‹eq-weapon/greatclub«s›). This benefit applies only to feats selected as fighter bonus feats." alter="Weapon training"}

two_handed_fighter.name: Two-Handed Fighter
description: ## Two-Handed Fighter
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/108›

Some fighters focus their efforts on finding the biggest, heaviest, most imposing weapon they can find and training to manage and harness the weight of their massive weapons for maximum impact. These fighting school benefits only apply when using two-handed weapons.

::ab[Shattering Strike (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A two-handed fighter gains a +1 bonus to CMB and CMD on ‹rule/sunder› attempts and on damage rolls made against objects." increment="p!These bonuses~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Overhand Chop (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="When a two-handed fighter makes a single attack (with the attack action or a ‹rule/charge›) with a two-handed weapon, he adds double his Strength bonus on damage rolls." replace="Armor training 1"}
::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=down ability="As the fighter class feature, but the bonuses only apply when wielding two-handed melee weapons."}

::ab[Backswing (Ex)]{l=7 icon=melee passive="When a two-handed fighter makes a ‹rule/full attack› with a two-handed weapon, he adds double his Strength bonus on damage rolls for all attacks after the first." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Piledriver (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee standard="A two-handed fighter can make a single melee attack with a two-handed weapon. If the attack hits, he may make a ‹rule/bull rush› or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver against the target of his attack as a @HLfree_action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Greater Power Attack (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost passive="When using ‹feat/Power Attack› with a two-handed melee weapon, the bonus damage from Power Attack is doubled (+100%) instead of increased by half (+50%)." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Devastating Blow (Ex)]{l=19 icon=melee standard="A two-handed fighter may make a single melee attack with a two-handed weapon at a -5 penalty. If the attack hits, it is treated as a critical threat." special="Special weapon abilities that activate only on a critical hit do not activate if this critical hit is confirmed." replace="Armor mastery"}

two_weapon_warrior.name: Two-Weapon Warrior
description: ## Two-Weapon Warrior
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/109›

Trained under great masters who preached the simple truth that two are better than one when it comes to weapons, the two-weapon warrior is a terror when his hands are full. From paired daggers to exotic double weapons, all combinations come equally alive in his skilled hands.

::ab[Defensive Flurry (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="When a two-weapon warrior makes a ‹rule/full attack› with both weapons, he gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC against melee attacks until the beginning of his next turn." increment="This bonus~3~4" replace="Armor training 1 and 2"}

::ab[Twin Blades (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A two-weapon warrior gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when making a full attack with two weapons or a double weapon." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Doublestrike (Ex)]{l=9 icon=melee standard="A two-weapon warrior may make one attack with both his primary and secondary weapons. The penalties for ‹rule/attacking with »two weapon«s<_fighting› apply normally." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Improved Balance (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost passive="The attack penalties for fighting with two weapons are reduced by -1 for a two-weapon warrior. Alternatively, he may use a one-handed weapon in his off-hand, treating it as if it were a light weapon with the normal light weapon penalties." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Equal Opportunity (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power ability="When a two-weapon warrior makes an attack of opportunity, he may attack once with both his primary and secondary weapons. The penalties for attacking with two weapons apply normally." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Perfect Balance (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost passive="The penalties for fighting with two weapons are reduced by an additional -1 for a two-weapon warrior. This benefit stacks with *improved balance.* If he is using a one-handed weapon in his off hand, treating it as a light weapon, he uses the normal light weapon penalties." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Deft Doublestrike (Ex)]{l=17 icon=melee immediate="When a two-weapon warrior hits an opponent with both weapons, he can make a ‹rule/disarm› or ‹rule/sunder› attempt (or ‹rule/trip›, if one or both weapons can be used to trip) against that opponent, and it does not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Deadly Defense (Ex)]{l=19 icon=power passive="When a two-weapon warrior makes a full attack with both weapons, every creature that hits him with a melee attack before the beginning of his next turn provokes an attack of opportunity from the warrior." replace="Armor mastery"}

unarmed_fighter.name: Unarmed Fighter
description: ## Unarmed Fighter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/48›

Not all fighters need weapons to make their mark on the world. The unarmed fighter picks up a weapon only rarely, and when he does, he prefers the weapons of the monk. There are even those who mistake them for monks, but these fighters enter the fight without ki, and instead have a tough perseverance that few can rival.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="An unarmed fighter is not proficient with medium armor, heavy armor, or shields. An unarmed fighter is proficient with all ‹class/monk› weapons, including exotic monk weapons."}

::ab[Unarmed Style]{l=1 icon=power ability="An unarmed fighter gains the ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› feat and any single ‹main/style feat› as a bonus feat. The unarmed fighter need not meet all the prerequisites of the style feat he chooses, but style feats that grant additional uses of the ‹feat/Elemental Fist› feat cannot be taken until the unarmed fighter has that feat." replace="The bonus feat at 1st level"}

::ab[Harsh Training (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="An unarmed fighter gains a +1 bonus on saving throws against effects that cause the exhausted, fatigued, or staggered conditions or temporary penalties to ability scores." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Tough Guy (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="An unarmed fighter gains DR/- equal to ‹misc/half› his fighter level against nonlethal damage or damage taken while he is ‹rule/grappled›." replace="Armor training 1"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="An unarmed fighter gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls with weapons in the monk and natural ‹ability/weapon groups›." increment="This bonus~5~4" replace="Weapon training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

::ab[Clever Wrestler (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def-power passive="An unarmed fighter takes no penalties to Dexterity or on attack rolls while grappled, and retains his Dexterity bonus to AC while pinning an opponent." order=pa ability="The unarmed fighter can make attacks of opportunity even when grappled and even against creatures attempting to ‹rule/grapple› him if the opponent has the ‹feat/Improved Grapple› feat or the ‹umr/grab› ability." replace="Armor training 2"}

::ab[Trick Throw (Ex)]{l=8 icon=melee immediate="When an unarmed fighter successfully ‹rule/trip«s› an opponent with an unarmed attack, he can attempt a ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver against that creature (before the opponent becomes prone)." provokes=No replace="The 8th-level bonus feat"}

::ab[Takedown (Ex)]{l=12 icon=melee swift="If an unarmed fighter succeeds on a ‹rule/drag› maneuver, he can attempt a trip maneuver against the same target." provokes=No imp15="He may do so after a successful grapple check." replace="The 12th-level bonus feat"}

::ab[Eye Gouge (Ex)]{l=13 icon=melee swift="If an unarmed fighter confirms a critical hit with his unarmed strike or begins his turn grappled, he may attempt a dirty trick maneuver to ‹misc/blind› his target." provokes=No special="A target more than one size category larger is unaffected." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Sucker Punch (Ex)]{l=17 icon=melee swift="When an unarmed fighter strikes a creature that is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC, or that he has successfully pinned with a grapple check, he can attempt a dirty trick or trip combat maneuver against that target." replace="Armor training 4"}
::ab[Sheer Toughness (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="An unarmed fighter becomes immune to nonlethal damage and the exhausted, fatigued, and staggered conditions." replace="Armor mastery"}
::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=info note="*Weapon mastery* applies to unarmed strikes only." alter="Weapon mastery"}

unbreakable.name: Unbreakable
description: ## Unbreakable
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/49›

The unbreakable is a warrior of indomitable will, unstoppable and implacable once he has set his mind upon a course of action. The unbreakable endures any trial to do what must be done, and when his mind is set toward a goal, nothing can stop him from achieving it, though many may try. He is a juggernaut and a zealot, supremely confident in his own abilities.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="An unbreakable is not proficient with tower shields."}
::ab[Tough as Nails]{icon=power ability="An unbreakable gains ‹feat/Endurance› and ‹feat/Diehard› as bonus feats." replace="The fighter's 1st-level bonus feat"}
::ab[Unflinching (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="An unbreakable gains a +1 bonus on Will saves against mind-affecting effects." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Bravery}

::ab[Heroic Recovery (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="An unbreakable gains the ‹feat/Heroic Recovery› feat as a bonus feat, if he does not have it already. If he already has this feat, the unbreakable can choose any ‹main/combat feat› instead." incrementPlain="He may use this~5~4" incrementDesc=feat incrementEnd=" times per day." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Heroic Defiance (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="An unbreakable gains the ‹feat/Heroic Defiance› feat as a bonus feat, if he does not have it already. If he already has this feat, the unbreakable can choose any combat feat instead." incrementPlain="He may use this~9~4" incrementDesc=feat incrementEnd=" times per day." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Quick Recovery (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost passive="An unbreakable needs only 15 minutes of rest or to be subject to a healing spell or effect to recover from the ‹misc/fatigued› condition." replace="Armor training 3"}

::ab[Stalwart (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="When an unbreakable succeeds on a Fortitude or Will save against a spell or spell-like ability that has a partial effect even on a successful save, he is completely unaffected by it." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Unlimited Endurance (Ex)]{l=15 icon=boost passive="When an unbreakable is ‹misc/exhausted›, he only suffers the effects of the fatigued condition instead, but does require 1 hour of rest to reduce this condition to the actual fatigued condition." replace="Armor training 4"}

::ab[Miraculous Recovery (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def-roll passive="When an unbreakable makes a saving throw to recover from an ongoing effect, he may roll twice and choose the better roll." replace="Weapon training 4"}

::ab[Unbreakable Mind (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="An unbreakable becomes nearly impossible to sway with honeyed words or magic. He gains immunity to mind-affecting effects." replace="Weapon mastery"}

ustalavic_duelist.name: Ustalavic Duelist
description: ## Ustalavic Duelist
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/47›

The University of Lepidstadt has developed a reputation for churning out fearsome duelists. At the end of each academic year, the students all gather together and duel one another with light blades. Each student duels one opponent after another until he is marked on the cheek by an adversary's blade. These "Lepidstadt scars" are recognized throughout the Inner Sea region and beyond as marks of their prowess. The Lepidstadt Style is one of fluid motion and precise thrusts.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=info note="An Ustalavic duelist is proficient with all simple and martial weapons and with all light and medium armor and shields (except tower shields)." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Duelist Stance (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="An Ustalavic duelist gains a dodge bonus to AC as long as he is wearing no armor or light armor, wields a melee weapon in one hand, and his other hand is empty. This dodge bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of his fighter level." replace="The fighter's 1st-level bonus feat"}

::ab[Duelist Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost choice="An Ustalavic duelist must select the light blades ‹ability/weapon_>group<s› for the weapon training class feature." order=cp passive="The Ustalavic duelist's weapon training bonus with light blades increases by 1 on attack and damage rolls for every 4 levels he possesses beyond 5th (to a maximum of +4 on attack and damage rolls at 17th level). If he is using his *duelist stance,* this damage bonus increases by 2." special="The Ustalavic duelist does not gain weapon training in any other groups as he increases in level." replace="Weapon training 1"}

::ab[Lepidstadt Thrust (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="When using ‹feat/Vital Strike›, ‹feat/Improved Vital Strike›, or ‹feat/Greater Vital Strike›, an Ustalavic duelist adds his Intelligence modifier to damage rolls (this bonus to damage is not multiplied on a critical hit). If the duelist confirms a critical hit while using one of these feats, he automatically deals maximum damage." replace="Weapon training 2"}

::ab[Science of the Blade (Ex)]{l=13 icon=melee ability="An Ustalavic duelist is able to apply his specialized knowledge of anatomy and physiology to his bladework. The duelist can make ‹rule/dirty trick›, ‹rule/disarm›, and ‹rule/trip› combat maneuvers using any one-handed piercing or slashing weapon." replace="Weapon training 3"}

::ab[Surgical Strike (Ex)]{l=17 icon=melee ability="When an Ustalavic duelist confirms a critical hit with a light or one-handed piercing or slashing weapon, he deals ‹rule/ability damage› in addition to the normal bonus damage from the critical hit. The duelist chooses which ability is damaged, and deals an amount of damage equal to the critical multiplier of the wielded weapon." replace="Weapon training 4"}

varisian_free_style_fighter.name: Varisian Free-Style Fighter
description: ## Varisian Free-Style Fighter
‹SOURCE Weapon Master's Handbook/7›

Free-style fighters are most common in Varisia, where their training - with multiple teachers, or perhaps instruction in the anything-goes mentality of the Price of Freedom school in Kaer Maga - allows them to blend fighting styles into a greater whole.
::cskill{gain=ea~kl lose=kd~ride}

::ab[Martial Flexibility (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A Varisian free-style fighter gains *martial flexibility* as per the ‹class/brawler› class feature, treating his fighter level as his brawler level for the purposes of this ability." replace="Bonus feats gained at 1st level, 6th level, 10th level, and 12th level~weapon training~weapon mastery"}

::ab[Free Fighting Style (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A Varisian freestyle fighter can freely mix two of the styles he knows into a more flexible style. A Varisian free-style fighter can have an additional ‹main/style feat› stance active simultaneously with his first style feat stance. He can enter all of his allowed stances as a @HLmove_action or one stance as a @HLswift_action." imp11="A Varisian free-style fighter can have the stances of up to two additional style feats active at the same time." imp15="A Varisian free-style fighter can have the stances of up to three additional style feats active at the same time." replace="Armor training"}

venomblade.name: Venomblade
description: ## Venomblade
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/5›
The venomblade has learned to take full advantage of his toxic spittle, blinding his targets and then rushing in to cut them down where they stand.
::prereq{r=Nagaji}
